title: Aureline public about-destination contract
description: Boundary schema for one about_destination_record consumed by About, Help, marketplace, issue/reporting, governance, contributing, community-handoff, release-notes, upgrade/hosted, and source-repository surfaces. Every consuming surface MUST quote the same closed destination-class, destination-role, route-state, account-requirement, data-exit boundary, support-prominence, and local-only parity vocabulary so the open-vs-managed boundary is described identically across the product. Redirected, archived, or replaced destinations MUST carry an explicit replacement_destination_ref so dead links degrade to a labeled successor instead of silently failing. Surfaces whose primary purpose is support, troubleshooting, issue reporting, or source transparency MUST NOT carry support_prominence_class = deprioritized_below_upgrade; upgrade-or-hosted prompts can never push a valid local/open destination off the surface. Account-required write or subscribe destinations MUST cite a local_only_fallback_ref when the row is part of a flow whose local use is still account-optional. Raw URLs, raw email addresses, raw chat-room URLs, raw rotation entries, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and bounded reviewable summaries only. Adding a new destination-class, destination-role, route-state, account-requirement, data-exit boundary, support-prominence, or local-only parity class is additive-minor and bumps about_destination_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

about_destination_schema_version.description: Integer schema version. Current value is 1.

opaque_ref.description: Opaque, stable identifier safe for live surfaces, support exports, and public-proof packets. Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw email addresses, raw chat-room URLs, raw secret material MUST NOT appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Bounded reviewable sentence. Raw URLs, raw page-body bytes, raw user identifiers, and raw secrets MUST NOT appear.

destination_class.description: Closed four-class destination-trust vocabulary. About, Help, marketplace, issue/reporting, governance, and contributing routes MUST reuse exactly these labels. A destination whose trust class is unresolved MUST be denied at build time rather than collapsing to official_public.

destination_role_class.description: Closed destination-role vocabulary shared across About, Help, marketplace, issue/reporting, governance, contributing, release-notes, upgrade-or-hosted, and community-handoff surfaces.

route_state_class.description: Closed route-state vocabulary. Redirected, archived, or replaced rows MUST cite an explicit replacement_destination_ref. A decommissioned row MUST cite a local_only_fallback_ref when one exists, so the dead link degrades to a labeled path instead of silently failing.

account_requirement_class.description: Closed account-requirement vocabulary. About, Help, marketplace, and source-repository rows whose local use is still account-optional MUST cite a local_only_fallback_ref whenever this row's account requirement is required_for_write, required_for_subscribe, or required_for_premium_hosted.

data_exit_boundary_class.description: Closed data-exit-boundary vocabulary disclosed before navigation. third_party_vendor destinations MUST carry external_public_browse or vendor_or_third_party_outbound; security_intake destinations MUST carry security_payloads_only; support_intake destinations MUST carry redacted_support_packet.

support_prominence_class.description: Closed prominence vocabulary. Support-oriented surfaces (troubleshooting, issue_tracker, support_intake, security_intake, source_repository, contributing_guide, status_page) MUST NOT carry below_upgrade — sponsorship and upgrade-or-hosted CTAs cannot push a valid support, source, or troubleshooting route off the surface.

local_only_parity_class.description: Closed local-only parity vocabulary. account_optional_local_parity and mixed_local_optional_account rows MUST keep a local/open destination reachable without account coercion. hosted_only_no_local_fallback rows MUST be labeled honestly and may not appear on About, Help, source-repository, or contributing surfaces when the same flow has an open or local alternative.

build_context_export_class.description: Closed build-context export-block vocabulary. Each destination row that opens an issue, support intake, security intake, or community discussion MUST cite at least one build_context_export_class so the lane can attach a versioned export block instead of a screenshot.

export_block_schema_version.description: Schema version of the export-block payload itself. About, public-issue templates, support intake, and community discussion MUST cite the same version when they quote the same block.

allOf.description: Redirected, archived, or replaced destinations MUST cite an explicit replacement_destination_ref so dead links degrade to a labeled successor.
Decommissioned routes MUST cite a local-only fallback so the dead link points to a discoverable replacement path.
third_party_vendor destinations MUST disclose their data-exit boundary as external public browse or vendor/third-party outbound.
security_intake destinations carry security_payloads_only data-exit boundary.
support_intake destinations carry redacted_support_packet data-exit boundary and an official_private trust class.
Support-oriented destination roles MUST NOT be deprioritized below upgrade or sponsorship CTAs.
Local-only fallback rows are always reachable without account coercion: account_requirement = none, support_prominence in {source_first, support_first, troubleshooting_first}.
Destinations whose account requirement is required_for_write, required_for_subscribe, or required_for_premium_hosted MUST cite a local-only fallback when local-only parity is still account-optional.

Issue-tracker, support-intake, security-intake, and community-handoff destinations MUST attach at least one build-context export block so the lane can carry a versioned export instead of a screenshot.
upgrade_or_hosted and sponsorship_or_funding destinations MUST NOT outrank source-repository or support routes on the same surface.
